title: Ethiopian Sirloin Stew
description: A flavorful and spicy Ethiopian-inspired stew made with sirloin and a variety of spices. This hearty dish is perfect for a chilly evening.

ingredients.name: Ethiopian
ingredients.amount: 2 tablespoons niter kibbeh or vegetable oil

instructions: Heat oil in a large pot over medium-high heat.
Add sirloin and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove from pot and set aside.
Add berbere spice mix and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add onion and garlic and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add carrots and potatoes and cook for 5 minutes.
Add beef broth and bring to a boil.
Return sirloin to pot and reduce heat to low.
Simmer, covered, until sirloin is cooked through and vegetables are tender, about 1 hour.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot over rice or with injera bread.
